skill.name: competitive-review
skill.description: Runs a structured competitive review by asking architecture and implementation reviewers to analyze the same complex coding task, then merges their findings before deeper reasoning.
skill.summary: Use competing architecture and implementation reviews before major coding decisions.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell-style backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance, but review found no executable code or cryptographic logic in SKILL.md. The command findings are markdown and text examples for agent orchestration, and the remaining concern is low-risk prompt steering of internal reviewers.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick locations are markdown or text code fences that show Task agent calls, report templates, and a skill flow diagram. No evidence found of Ruby execution, shell execution, command construction, or user-controlled command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is entirely inside fenced documentation blocks, and the file contains no runnable script body. The static pattern is present, but semantic context confirms it is not command execution.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Not Present
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the YAML description field. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption code, hashing code, or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain skill metadata and do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithm names. This is a high-confidence false positive.

low_findings.title: Low-Risk Competitive Prompt Steering
low_findings.description: The skill tells subagents they are competing and uses promotion framing to encourage thoroughness. This is not system reconnaissance, but it may bias reviewer behavior or create unnecessary urgency in analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt steering text is explicit and visible in the skill instructions, but it does not request secrets, privileged access, or policy bypass. The concern is behavioral quality, not confirmed malicious activity.

content.value_statement: Complex coding work can miss architecture risks, implementation bugs, and unverified assumptions. This skill coordinates competing reviewer perspectives and merges their findings before deeper analysis.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to run a competitive review before complex coding tasks.
Specifies parallel architecture and implementation reviewer roles.
Provides a format for comparing reviewer results by severity.
Requires deduplication of combined issues before deeper reasoning.
Feeds verified facts and merged findings into a follow-up deep analysis step.
limitations: Requires access to compatible reviewer agents or equivalent review roles.
Does not implement automated code scanning or static analysis by itself.
May add overhead for small tasks that do not need multiple reviewers.
Reviewer quality depends on the context and files provided by the user.

use_cases.title: Review a Risky Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Compare architecture and implementation concerns before building a feature with multiple dependencies.

use_cases.title: Validate a Refactor Strategy
use_cases.description: Surface structural risks, code-level regressions, and missing facts before changing an existing system.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Decision Review
use_cases.description: Collect competing technical arguments before choosing a framework, pattern, or migration path.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a single complex coding request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use competitive-review on this task. Compare architecture and implementation risks before recommending a solution: [describe task].

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Code Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when files or a pull request need balanced review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a competitive review of these changes. Have one reviewer focus on architecture and one on implementation correctness, then merge findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Technical Decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before selecting an approach with tradeoffs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use competitive-review for this decision: [decision]. Compare long-term design risks, implementation risks, and facts that need verification.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Review Before Execution
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before starting a high-impact implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run competitive-review, deduplicate findings, identify the strongest objections, verify key facts, and feed the result into a deeper implementation plan.

output_examples.input: Review a plan to add a shared authentication middleware across three services.
output: A comparison table showing each reviewer and the number of high, medium, and low issues found.
A deduplicated list of architecture and implementation risks.
A short set of verified facts to carry into the final plan.

output_examples.input: Check whether a proposed refactor should move validation into a shared package.
output: Architecture concerns about coupling, ownership, and versioning.
Implementation concerns about migration order, tests, and compatibility.
A winner selection based on severity, followed by combined recommendations.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether to use a new language feature in production code.
output: Fact checks about language and runtime support.
Risks from both maintainability and implementation perspectives.
A concise decision brief for deeper analysis.

best_practices: Use the skill for complex tasks where missing risks would be costly.
Provide the same complete context to both reviewer roles.
Deduplicate findings before using them in the final recommendation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for trivial edits that need a direct implementation.
Do not treat reviewer counts as a substitute for verifying evidence.
Do not pass sensitive context unless all reviewer agents are approved for it.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It coordinates two reviewer perspectives, compares their findings, and merges the results before deeper technical analysis.

faq.question: Does it run code or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a written review protocol and contains no executable scripts.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before complex coding, architecture changes, technical decisions, or codebase questions with meaningful risk.

faq.question: Can it replace human code review?
faq.answer: No. It can improve preparation and analysis, but human review is still needed for important changes.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the task, relevant files, constraints, and the decision you need to make.
